rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF RICHARD PATUREL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/15/21. Notice still has not been given to the conservatee. [Probate Code Section 14.60(b)(2).] Neither the amended petition nor the supplement to it addresses the taking of fees without a Court order. [CRC 7.755.] The register of actions does not show any order that authorized the conservator to pay himself up to eight hours per month. The accounting reflects what appear to be duplicate disbursements of allowance to the conservatee, and a substantial payment for attendant care on 4/10/19, which has not yet been explained. A copy of the invoice from Family Care SF, Inc., must be filed. No amended Letters have been filed yet that conforms to the amended order dated 2/25/2019, as noted by the Court Investigator. If these issues are not addressed in time to deliver courtesy copies of the responsive papers to the assigned Examiner by 6/4/21, an appearance will be required on 6/15/21.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ARTHUR LAM
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although no proper, endorsed filed copy of the supplement was provided to the Examiner, the "modifications" in the "supplement" actually amended the petition (see CRC 7.3 regarding supplements or amendments to petitions), it is unclear if the proposed distribution reflects payment of the attorney fees and costs, and the total of the attorney costs appears to be incorrect. Since the two co-administrators are the sole heirs-at-law, these defects will be overlooked in order for the estate to close. No revised proposed order has been submitted to reflect the amendments. NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate a proceeding must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution," or "Order Terminating Conservatorship [or Guardianship or Trust].â" [LRSF 14.36 I.6.b.] Please make a note of it. (Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).])

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/22/2021. 1) No courtesy copy of the objection has been received. 2) No proposed order from the objector has been received. 3) If there is no personal representative of the trustor's estate, each heir identified in the supplement must be served in accordance with Probate Code section 851(a)(2) which requires the same manner of service of a summons in a civil action. 4) Notice must be given on a mandatory Judicial Council form DE-115. 5) A copy of the supplement must be served on all parties entitled to notice.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE CHARLES L. WEYRO TESTAMENTARY TRUST DATED MARCH 31, 1976
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Appointing Successor Trustee And For Instructions
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review the 9th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 9th amended general order.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MICHAEL AQUINO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 9th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their full name. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Court to determine how to proceed. 2) Examiner notes: a. Petitioner did not select a box on page 1 under 1 and did not specify the name of the newspaper. b. On page 1 #2 under name of Petitioner is blank. c. Please file a copy of Decedent's death certificate. d. On page 1 under #2 c. petitioner did not check a box full or limited authority. e. On page 1 under #2 d. Petitioner selected bond not be required for the reasons stated in item 3e and then under 3e. petitioner did not select a box. f. On page 1 under #3.a Petitioner left place of death blank. g. On page 2 under 3. D. Petitioner states the personal property is $2,700,000.00 and it appears Petitioner alleges the annual gross income from real property is $6,000.00. Please explain why the subtotal adds up to $2,707,200.00. Please explain why gross fair market value of real property is blank if there is $6,000.00 in annual gross income from real property. Please explain why net value of real property is blank. h. Petitioner selected Probate of Lost Will and for Letters Testamentary and then selected on page 2 under #3 f. Decedent died intestate. Please explain. i. On page 2 under #3h petitioner did not select boxes 1 resident of California or 2 nonresident of California and 3 resident of United States or 4 nonresident of United States have not been selected. j. Please explain why Petitioner did not select a box on page 3 under #5 a (1) spouse or (2) no spouse) when there is a spouse listed on page 4 under #8. k. Please explain why Petitioner did not include an Attachment 3(f)(3) that includes a statement of the testamentary words or their substance and states reasons in that attachment why the presumption in Probate Code 6124 does not apply. l. Petitioner has not filed Notice of Petition to Administer Estate (DE-121). m. Petitioner has not filed Duties and Liabilities of Personal Representative (DE-147). n. Petitioner has not filed Proof of Publication. o. A hard copy of the proposed order must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. (LRSF 14.93). [Proposed orders may not be e-filed.]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE DANIEL GOMEZ URIONAGUENA & MANUELA MUNOZ DE GOMEZ REV. LIVING TRUST, U/T/A DTD 6/29/94 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Determining Title To Certain Assets
rulings.Rulings: continued to 6/1/21. no later than noon on 5/25/21, petitioners must file a memorandum of points and authorities making clear the legal basis for filing this petition as a trust petition, as the relief sought is to confirm a bank account titled in the name of a decedent to three individuals. if the alternative relief sought is to confirm the bank account as a trust asset, please provide the legal basis for this court to find that a bank account titled in the name of a business, and not listed on schedule a, could be viewed as a trust asset. what action was taken by the trustors that created a trust over this account? and if the end result is that the account is a trust asset, on what basis could the court confirm ownership in anyone other than the trustees? please note, this court does not accept the argument that having a pour-over will is sufficient to confirm assets titled in the name of a decedent are trust assets.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KENNETH CARMAN WRIGHT
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties must, whenever possible, appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review the Court's 9th Amended General Order which took effect on 02/10/2021 for more information.
